commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because I found no actionable patch defect and the contributor supplied after-fix runtime proof.; Cleared: The diff touches local state-file persistence only, adds no dependency or workflow execution surface, preserves private 0600 file creation, and keeps approval-index no-overwrite semantics. (sha=3deff0efb07226293445bacf81e13d3761a0d0af)
commands.review_summary:
The branch routes diff snapshot writes through atomic storage, treats malformed diff and approval-index JSON as missing state, adds an exclusive approval-index writer with hard-link and direct wx fallback paths, and adds regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main parses malformed diff snapshots and approval-index files before it can recover, while only ENOENT is handled in the affected read paths. I did not run a live SIGKILL harness under the read-only review constraint.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because I found no actionable patch defect and the contributor supplied after-fix runtime proof.

target.title: fix: harden diff and approval state writes
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat corrupt LLM cache entries, diff snapshots, and approval ID index entries as disposable misses instead of crashing later runs or resume-by-ID.
- Write cache, diff, and state data through same-directory atomic replacement with file fsync, parent-directory sync, private new-file modes, and deterministic cleanup on failed publish paths.
- Preserve approval ID no-overwrite semantics with hard-link exclusive publish; filesystems that cannot publish the short-ID index atomically fall back to full resume-token approval instead of risking a partial index.
- Keep authoritative resume state strict: malformed resume state still surfaces as malformed JSON instead of being silently hidden.
- Add maintainer changelog credit for @TurboTheTurtle covering issues #111, #112, and #113.

## Proof
Runtime proof from the final built CLI:

```text
diff-exit=0
diff-stdout=[
  {
    "kind": "diff.last",
    "key": "snapshot",
    "changed": true,
    "before": null,
    "after": {
      "ok": true
    }
  }
]
diff-stderr=
approval-exit=2
approval-stdout={
  "protocolVersion": 1,
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "type": "parse_error",
    "message": "Approval ID \"deadbeef\" not found or expired"
  }
}
approval-stderr=
```

## Validation
- `node --test dist/test/state.test.js dist/test/approval_id.test.js dist/test/llm_task_invoke.test.js dist/test/llm_invoke.test.js` (36 tests passed)
- `pnpm --config.verifyDepsBeforeRun=false run lint` (passed; existing warnings only)
- `pnpm --config.verifyDepsBeforeRun=false run test` (255 tests passed)
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-skills/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

Note: local pnpm script runs used `verifyDepsBeforeRun=false` because the latest base commit updates `@typescript/native-preview` to packages that currently trip pnpm's minimum-release-age pre-run policy on this machine; the lockfile was not changed by this PR.
target.author: turbotheturtle
